text: Find the red sneakers shown in the homepage banner image and add them to the shopping cart in size 10.
screenshots:
Screenshot 1: Homepage with large hero banner showing a lifestyle photo of a runner wearing red Nike Air Max sneakers on a trail. Navigation bar at top with categories: Men, Women, Kids, Sale. Search bar in upper right. Below the banner, a grid of 12 featured products with thumbnail images and prices.

Screenshot 2: Search results page showing 8 sneaker products. The agent has typed 'red sneakers' in the search bar. Results include red Nike Air Max ($129.99), red Adidas Ultraboost ($149.99), red Puma RS-X ($89.99), and others. Each result has a product image, name, price, and star rating.

Screenshot 3: Product detail page for Nike Air Max in red colorway. Large product image on left showing the shoe from side angle. Right side has title, price ($129.99), color selector with red highlighted, size dropdown menu expanded showing sizes 8-13, and 'Add to Cart' button.

Screenshot 4: Same product page with size 10 selected in the dropdown. The 'Add to Cart' button is highlighted. A mini-cart popup in the upper right shows '1 item' with the red Nike Air Max thumbnail.
trajectory:
Step 1: Observe homepage banner — identify red sneakers in hero image
Step 2: Click search bar in upper right corner
Step 3: Type 'red sneakers' and press Enter
Step 4: Scan search results — identify Nike Air Max matching banner image
Step 5: Click on Nike Air Max product listing
Step 6: Click size dropdown menu
Step 7: Select size '10' from dropdown options
Step 8: Click 'Add to Cart' button
Step 9: STOP — Task complete
final_state: Product page for Nike Air Max (Red) displayed. Size 10 selected. Cart indicator shows 1 item. Mini-cart popup confirms: Nike Air Max - Red - Size 10 - $129.99. Cart total: $129.99.

text: On the travel website, find a hotel that looks similar to the one shown in the reference image (a beachfront resort with a pool) and book it for 2 nights.
screenshots:
Screenshot 1: Travel homepage with search form. A reference image is displayed at the top showing a tropical beachfront resort — white multi-story building, infinity pool facing the ocean, palm trees lining a white sand beach, thatched-roof cabanas by the pool.

Screenshot 2: Hotel search results for 'Miami Beach' showing 6 hotels in a grid layout. Each has a large photo, name, star rating, price per night. Hotels include: The Palms Resort (beachfront with pool, $289/night), Ocean View Inn (city view, $159/night), Seaside Boutique (small beach, no pool, $199/night), Grand Beach Resort (large beachfront with infinity pool, $349/night).

Screenshot 3: The agent has clicked on Grand Beach Resort. Detail page shows a photo carousel with 8 images — the main photo shows a white building with an infinity pool overlooking the ocean, closely matching the reference image. Room selection form visible below with date pickers and room type options.

Screenshot 4: Booking confirmation page showing Grand Beach Resort, Deluxe Ocean View room, 2 nights, total $698. Check-in and check-out dates filled. Guest name form partially visible.
trajectory:
Step 1: View reference image of beachfront resort with pool
Step 2: Enter 'Miami Beach' in destination search field
Step 3: Set check-in date to next available date
Step 4: Set check-out date to 2 days later
Step 5: Click 'Search' button
Step 6: Scan hotel results — compare photos to reference image
Step 7: Click on Grand Beach Resort (best visual match — beachfront, white building, infinity pool)
Step 8: Review photo carousel to confirm visual similarity
Step 9: Select 'Deluxe Ocean View' room type
Step 10: Click 'Book Now' button
Step 11: STOP — Task complete
final_state: Booking confirmation for Grand Beach Resort displayed. Room: Deluxe Ocean View. Duration: 2 nights. Total: $698. The selected hotel visually matches the reference image — beachfront location, white building, infinity pool facing the ocean.

text: Navigate to the forum and reply to the thread that contains a chart showing declining user engagement metrics.
screenshots:
Screenshot 1: Forum homepage listing 15 recent threads. Thread titles visible include 'Q4 Performance Review', 'New Feature Request: Dark Mode', 'User Engagement Analysis — Concerning Trends', 'Bug Report: Login Issues', 'Welcome New Members'. Each thread shows title, author avatar, reply count, and last activity timestamp.

Screenshot 2: Inside the thread 'User Engagement Analysis — Concerning Trends'. The original post contains a line chart showing Monthly Active Users declining from 45,000 in January to 28,000 in June. Below the chart is a bar graph showing average session duration dropping from 12 minutes to 7 minutes. The post author discusses possible causes.

Screenshot 3: The agent has scrolled to the bottom of the thread. A reply text box is visible with the agent's cursor in it. The text area shows the agent has typed a response about the engagement decline.

Screenshot 4: The reply has been posted. It appears as the latest comment in the thread, showing the agent's response text with a timestamp of 'Just now'.
trajectory:
Step 1: Scan forum homepage for threads containing chart references
Step 2: Identify 'User Engagement Analysis — Concerning Trends' as likely match
Step 3: Click on the thread title
Step 4: Scroll through post — observe line chart showing declining MAU and bar graph showing declining session duration
Step 5: Scroll to bottom of thread to find reply box
Step 6: Click on reply text area
Step 7: Type response discussing the engagement metrics
Step 8: Click 'Reply' button
Step 9: STOP — Task complete
final_state: Reply successfully posted in 'User Engagement Analysis — Concerning Trends' thread. The agent correctly identified the thread containing the declining engagement chart. Reply appears as the latest comment with 'Just now' timestamp.

text: On the recipe site, find the dish that visually matches this description: a pasta dish with green pesto sauce and pine nuts on top. Save it to your favorites.
screenshots:
Screenshot 1: Recipe site homepage showing a grid of 16 recipe cards with food photos. Visible dishes include: spaghetti carbonara (creamy yellow sauce), grilled salmon, Caesar salad, penne with pesto (bright green sauce with scattered pine nuts on top), mushroom risotto, chicken tikka masala, chocolate lava cake, and others.

Screenshot 2: The agent has clicked on 'Classic Basil Pesto Penne'. Recipe detail page shows a large hero image of penne pasta coated in vibrant green pesto sauce, topped with toasted pine nuts and fresh basil leaves. Recipe details: prep time 15 min, cook time 12 min, serves 4. Ingredients list and heart-shaped 'Save to Favorites' button visible.

Screenshot 3: The agent has clicked the heart icon. It is now filled red, and a toast notification at the bottom reads 'Recipe saved to favorites!' The recipe page remains displayed with the filled heart icon.
trajectory:
Step 1: Scan homepage recipe grid — look for pasta with green pesto and pine nuts
Step 2: Identify 'Classic Basil Pesto Penne' — image shows green pesto sauce with pine nuts
Step 3: Click on the recipe card
Step 4: Verify hero image matches description — green pesto, pine nuts visible
Step 5: Locate 'Save to Favorites' heart button
Step 6: Click heart icon to save
Step 7: STOP — Task complete
final_state: Recipe page for 'Classic Basil Pesto Penne' displayed. Heart icon is filled red indicating saved status. Toast notification confirms 'Recipe saved to favorites!' The recipe image matches the target description: pasta with green pesto sauce and pine nuts on top.

text: Find the product listing where the customer review photo shows a defective item (cracked screen), and report the listing.
screenshots:
Screenshot 1: Marketplace category page for 'Tablets' showing 8 product listings. Each has a main product image, title, price, and star rating. Products range from $199 to $899. The agent is browsing the listings.

Screenshot 2: Product page for 'ProTab X10 Tablet — 10.1 inch'. Main product image shows a pristine tablet. Below, the reviews section is visible with 4.2 stars average. The agent has scrolled to customer reviews.

Screenshot 3: Customer reviews section expanded. Review by 'frustrated_buyer' (1 star) includes a photo showing the tablet with a clearly visible crack running diagonally across the screen from upper left to lower right. The crack splinters into web-like fractures near the center. Review text reads: 'Arrived broken! Screen was cracked right out of the box.'

Screenshot 4: Report dialog is open over the listing page. The agent has selected 'Defective/Damaged Product' as the report reason. A text field shows additional details about the customer review photo evidence. A 'Submit Report' button is at the bottom.
trajectory:
Step 1: Browse tablet category listings
Step 2: Click on 'ProTab X10 Tablet' listing
Step 3: Scroll down to customer reviews section
Step 4: Scan review photos — identify cracked screen in review by 'frustrated_buyer'
Step 5: Click 'Report Listing' button
Step 6: Select 'Defective/Damaged Product' as report reason
Step 7: Type details referencing the customer review photo evidence
Step 8: Click 'Submit Report'
Step 9: STOP — Task complete
final_state: Report submitted for ProTab X10 Tablet listing. Report reason: Defective/Damaged Product. The agent correctly identified the review photo showing a cracked screen and reported the listing. Confirmation message: 'Report submitted — our team will review within 24 hours.'

text: In the project management tool, find the Gantt chart showing the delayed 'Backend API' task and reassign it to developer Sarah Chen.
screenshots:
Screenshot 1: Project dashboard showing 'Project Phoenix' overview. Left sidebar has navigation: Board, Backlog, Timeline, Reports. Summary cards show: 12 In Progress, 5 Blocked, 23 Completed. The agent clicks 'Timeline' in the sidebar.

Screenshot 2: Gantt chart / Timeline view displayed. Horizontal bars represent tasks across a 3-month timeline. Most bars are green (on track) or blue (completed). One bar labeled 'Backend API Development' is red, extending 2 weeks past its original end date marked by a dashed vertical line. Assigned to: Mike Johnson.

Screenshot 3: Task detail modal for 'Backend API Development' is open. Shows: Status: In Progress, Assignee: Mike Johnson (with avatar), Due Date: Mar 15 (overdue, highlighted red), Priority: High. An 'Assignee' dropdown is expanded showing team members: Sarah Chen, Mike Johnson, Alex Rivera, Priya Patel.

Screenshot 4: Task detail updated. Assignee now shows 'Sarah Chen' with her avatar. A change log entry at the bottom reads: 'Assignee changed from Mike Johnson to Sarah Chen'. The Gantt chart in the background still shows the red bar for Backend API.
trajectory:
Step 1: Navigate to Project Phoenix dashboard
Step 2: Click 'Timeline' in left sidebar to view Gantt chart
Step 3: Scan Gantt chart — identify red bar indicating delay
Step 4: Click on 'Backend API Development' red bar
Step 5: In task detail modal, click Assignee dropdown
Step 6: Select 'Sarah Chen' from team member list
Step 7: Confirm reassignment
Step 8: STOP — Task complete
final_state: Task 'Backend API Development' reassigned from Mike Johnson to Sarah Chen. Task detail modal shows updated assignee with change log confirmation. Gantt chart still shows the task as delayed (red bar extending past deadline).

text: On the real estate site, find the listing that has a virtual tour showing a kitchen with granite countertops and stainless steel appliances, and schedule a viewing.
screenshots:
Screenshot 1: Real estate search results page showing 6 property listings in a grid. Each listing has a main photo, address, price, bed/bath count, and square footage. Prices range from $350,000 to $725,000. A map view is visible on the right showing pin locations.

Screenshot 2: The agent has clicked on '42 Maple Drive — $485,000'. Listing detail page shows a photo carousel. The current photo shows the exterior — a two-story colonial house with a blue front door. Navigation dots indicate 12 photos. A 'Virtual Tour' button is prominent below the carousel.

Screenshot 3: Virtual tour view of the kitchen. Panoramic photo showing: dark granite countertops with subtle gold flecking, stainless steel refrigerator (French door style), stainless steel oven range, stainless steel dishwasher, white shaker cabinets, subway tile backsplash, pendant lights over a center island with bar seating.

Screenshot 4: 'Schedule a Viewing' modal overlaying the listing page. Calendar widget shows available time slots. The agent has selected a date and time. Form fields include name, email, phone, and a message box. 'Confirm Viewing' button at bottom.
trajectory:
Step 1: Browse real estate listings
Step 2: Click on first listing to check virtual tour
Step 3: Click 'Virtual Tour' button
Step 4: Navigate virtual tour — look for kitchen with granite countertops and stainless steel appliances
Step 5: Confirm kitchen matches description — granite countertops, stainless steel fridge, oven, dishwasher
Step 6: Close virtual tour, click 'Schedule a Viewing' button
Step 7: Select available date and time
Step 8: Fill in contact information
Step 9: Click 'Confirm Viewing'
Step 10: STOP — Task complete
final_state: Viewing scheduled for 42 Maple Drive. Confirmation displayed with date, time, and property address. The virtual tour confirmed the kitchen has granite countertops and stainless steel appliances matching the task description.

text: On the social media dashboard, find the post with the highest engagement rate from last week and create a similar follow-up post using the same visual style.
screenshots:
Screenshot 1: Social media analytics dashboard. Top section shows weekly overview: Total Posts: 14, Total Reach: 52,300, Average Engagement: 4.2%. Below is a table of posts sorted by engagement rate. Top post: 'Summer Collection Launch' with an image post showing flat-lay product photography on a pastel pink background — 8.7% engagement, 12,400 reach, 1,078 likes, 234 comments.

Screenshot 2: Post detail view for 'Summer Collection Launch'. Large preview of the image: products arranged in a symmetrical flat-lay on pastel pink background with soft shadows, clean sans-serif text overlay reading 'NEW: Summer Collection'. Engagement metrics: 8.7% engagement rate, 1,078 likes, 234 comments, 89 shares. Hashtags: #SummerStyle #NewCollection.

Screenshot 3: Post creation interface. The agent has opened the 'Create Post' form. A text area contains draft caption text. The 'Upload Image' section shows a newly created image following the same flat-lay style on pastel pink background, this time with fall products and text overlay reading 'COMING SOON: Fall Collection'. Preview visible on the right.

Screenshot 4: Post preview and scheduling screen. The new post is shown as it would appear in the feed. Caption, image, and hashtags are all visible. A 'Publish' button and 'Schedule' option are at the bottom. The agent has clicked 'Publish'.
trajectory:
Step 1: View analytics dashboard — examine engagement metrics
Step 2: Identify 'Summer Collection Launch' as highest engagement post (8.7%)
Step 3: Click on post to view full details and visual style
Step 4: Note visual style: flat-lay, pastel pink background, clean text overlay
Step 5: Click 'Create Post' button
Step 6: Compose follow-up caption referencing fall collection
Step 7: Upload image matching the flat-lay pastel style
Step 8: Add relevant hashtags
Step 9: Click 'Publish'
Step 10: STOP — Task complete
final_state: New post 'COMING SOON: Fall Collection' published successfully. Post uses same visual style as highest-engagement post: flat-lay product photography on pastel pink background with clean text overlay. Confirmation message displayed.
